items.title: Lyse Doucet: Historic US-Iran talks must bridge deep distrust
items.summary: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

items.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán senses victory
items.summary: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

items.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph - now comes the hard part
items.summary: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

items.title: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
items.summary: The congressman is a leading Democratic candidate who is now facing calls to drop out of the race.

items.title: Melania Trump's speech propels Epstein crisis back to forefront
items.summary: She has now placed herself squarely into the Epstein story and at odds with the administration, which wants to end the investigation.

items.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
items.summary: The 61-year-old denies threatening physical violence and plying his wife with drugs to force her into having sex with strangers.

items.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
items.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

items.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
items.summary: The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years.

items.title: Trump posts graphic video of slaying to argue for stricter immigration policies
items.summary: A Haitian migrant previously protected from deportation is acccused of beating a woman to death at a Florida petrol station.

items.title: Irish government prepares fuel support deal after blockade pressure
items.summary: Travel across parts of the Republic of Ireland was affected for the fourth day in a row as vehicles, including tractors, blocked roads.

items.title: South Korea deploys thermal imaging cameras in search for escaped zoo wolf
items.summary: The male wolf, named Neukgu, escaped a zoo in the central city of Daejeon on Wednesday, officials said.

items.title: Gunmen kill at least four people at Afghanistan picnic spot
items.summary: Officials say attackers opened fire near the village of Deh Mehri in Herat.

items.title: Dutch police urge victims of 'international sextortion campaign' to come forward
items.summary: Dutch police have warned that dozens of women and girls were coerced into degrading sexual acts on camera in several countries.

items.title: French man charged with locking son in van for more than a year
items.summary: The boy was found malnourished, naked, lying next to human excrement and unable to walk, the prosecutor said.

items.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
items.summary: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

items.title: 'Every drop of water counts': Fear for the future of Argentina's glaciers
items.summary: A controversial law to remove protections for the country's glaciers has passed, opening the doors for mining.

items.title: Trump has handed JD Vance his most difficult mission yet
items.summary: As the US vice-president leads peace talks with Iran, he must balance warring factions, a demanding boss and his own political future.

items.title: An inappropriate joke nearly ended his career. Now he's back with more humour
items.summary: A misfired joke nearly derailed Samay Raina’s comedy career. Now he’s back, reclaiming his voice.

items.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
items.summary: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

items.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
items.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

items.title: The five big sticking points in US-Iran talks
items.summary: Peace talks between the US and Iran are due to begin in Pakistan on Saturday.

items.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
items.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.

items.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
items.summary: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

items.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
items.summary: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

items.title: Watch: Pope Leo XIV spins basketball on his finger, with help from Harlem Globetrotters
items.summary: US exhibition squad, the Harlem Globetrotters, met the pontiff in the Vatican, helping him to spin a basketball on the tip of his finger.
